title: ECDH, X25519, and Authenticated Key Agreement · Execution Trace: Crypto Go X25519 Choice
summary: A device protocol treats ECDH output as an encryption key, never authenticates peer keys, accepts malformed encodings, and reuses ephemeral keys indefinitely. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the x25519 choice increment in authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Select X25519 or approved NIST curves from protocol, hardware, compliance, interoperability, and implementation constraints.
Use supported ECDH curves through Go interfaces with private/public key separation, strict parsing, and shared-secret error handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go X25519 Choice · Theory Crypto Go X25519 Choice
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 5384 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
